summary: E2E validation of OnPar Live multi-player Match (replaces 1v1 Quick Match). Backend pytest 14/14 PASS covering create/validate/respond/active/get for stroke (3 players), match_play (2 players), best_ball (4 players + teams), decline-all → cancelled, rounds→scorecards mirror, and result computation per format. Legacy /api/matches/1v1/* routes still respond (no regression). Frontend E2E (mobile 390x844): Dashboard quick-match (renamed 'Match') → /match/new → fills opponents/course/tee/holes → POST /api/matches → navigates to /play/{course}?match={id}. Buddy entering /match/{id} sees Accept/Decline, clicking Accept → backend status=active and redirects to /play. Best Ball UI logic verified via code review (format-best_ball toggle + 4 opponent rows reveals 4 team1-toggle buttons enforcing exactly 2). Automated multi-opponent add via Playwright was flaky due to debounced search timing, but the addOpponent/toggleTeam1 logic in NewMatch.js is correct and the format-mismatch-warn ('Best Ball needs exactly 4 players.') renders properly while incomplete.

issues: Sequential multi-opponent add through search is debounced 250ms — works for human users but flaky for automated tests. Consider exposing add-opponent via stable id + ensure search input clears synchronously after addOpponent (currently does, just feels racy).

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~3828 lines — the new /api/matches block (~340 lines) plus legacy /api/matches/1v1 (~250 lines) both live alongside tournaments/feed/etc. Strongly recommend extracting routers/matches.py and routers/matches_legacy.py.

_match_compute_result is called inside GET /api/matches/{id}; it persists 'completed' on read which is the only place it gets computed — fine for now but consider also computing on POST /api/rounds finish=true so the 'completed' transition is not gated by someone visiting the detail page.

best_ball validation only requires team1 length===2 in UI; backend correctly checks both teams have 2. Good. UI could surface 'Team 2 will be the others' more prominently (already shown but small text).

GET /api/matches/active returns at most 50 docs sorted by created_at desc — fine for current scale, but if a user has many cancelled invites this could grow; consider also filtering to status pending/active (already done) and capping per status.
test_credentials: admin@fairway.com / FairwayAdmin123!; buddy@test.com, b2@test.com, b3@test.com all with BuddyTest123!. All four exist & login OK.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed needed. Tests pre/post clean tournaments where is_match=true and scorecards where tournament_id starts with tm_.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend Match endpoints fully covered in /app/backend/tests/test_match_multi.py (14 tests, all green). For Playwright Best Ball UI, prefer fewer sequential adds — programmatically POST /api/matches via API and validate UI rendering at /match/{id}. The 'add_init_script' approach overrides localStorage on every navigation, so to swap users mid-flow, drop the original add_init_script and explicitly reset before each goto.

backend: POST /api/matches stroke 3 players → pending, creator accepted, invitees pending
GET /api/matches/active visible to creator + every invited player
GET /api/matches/{id} returns slim card per player
Stroke 3-player result: lowest total wins; mirror creates scorecards with status=submitted
Match Play 2-player result: hole-by-hole 1/0.5/0 with score 'X–Y'
Best Ball 4-player 2-team result: per-hole min, team with more hole-wins gets winner_team
frontend: Opponent search → click opponent-option-{uid} adds row with remove-opponent-{uid}
Course + tee + holes selectable; match-send-btn enables when valid
Accept → backend status=active and redirect to /play
